text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: Bad teeth can be painful and worse.
zh: 牙齿不好可能会带来疼痛，甚至更糟的后果。

text: They can even be deadly.
zh: 它们甚至可能致命。

text: Infections of the gums and teeth can release bacteria into the blood system.
zh: 牙龈和牙齿的感染会将细菌释放到血液系统中。

text: Those bacteria can increase the chances of a heart attack or stroke and worsen the effects of other diseases.
zh: 这些细菌会增加心脏病发作或中风的几率，并加重其他疾病的病情。

text: And adults are not the only ones at risk.
zh: 而且，成年人并不是唯一有风险的人群。

text: For example, in 2007, doctors in the Washington area said a boy died when a tooth infection spread to his brain.
zh: 例如，2007年，华盛顿地区的医生表示，一名男孩因牙齿感染扩散到大脑而死亡。

text: They said it might have been prevented had he received the dental care he needed.
zh: 他们说，如果他及时得到所需的牙科护理，本可以避免这种情况。

text: He was 12 years old.
zh: 他当时只有12岁。

text: Experts at the National Institutes of Health say good dental care starts at birth.
zh: 美国国立卫生研究院的专家表示，良好的牙齿护理应从出生就开始。

text: Breast milk, they say, is the best food for the healthy development of teeth.
zh: 他们说，母乳是牙齿健康发育的最佳食物。

text: Breast milk can help slow bacterial growth and acid production in the mouth.
zh: 母乳有助于减缓口腔中细菌的生长和酸的产生。

text: But dentists say a baby's gums and early teeth should be cleaned after each feeding.
zh: 但牙医表示，婴儿的牙龈和早期长出的牙齿应在每次喂食后清洁。

text: Use a cloth with a little warm water.
zh: 用蘸有少量温水的布擦拭即可。

text: Do the same if a baby is fed with a bottle.
zh: 如果婴儿是用奶瓶喂养，也应同样清洁。

text: Experts say if you decide to put your baby to sleep with a bottle, only give the child water.
zh: 专家表示，如果你想让宝宝含着奶瓶入睡，瓶里只能装水。

text: When baby teeth begin to appear, here, you can clean them with a wet toothbrush.
zh: 当宝宝的乳牙开始长出时，可以用湿润的牙刷清洁。

text: Dentists say it is important to find soft toothbrushes made especially for babies and to use them very gently.
zh: 牙医表示，重要的是要选用专为婴儿设计的软毛牙刷，并且动作要非常轻柔。

text: The use of fluoride to protect teeth is common in many parts of the world.
zh: 使用氟化物保护牙齿在世界许多地方都很普遍。

text: For example, it is often added to drinking water supplies.
zh: 例如，氟化物常被添加到饮用水中。

text: The fluoride mixes with enamel, the hard surface on teeth, to help prevent holes or cavities from forming.
zh: 氟会与牙齿表面坚硬的牙釉质结合，有助于防止蛀牙或龋洞的形成。

text: But young children often swallow toothpaste when they brush.
zh: 但幼儿刷牙时常常会把牙膏吞下去。

text: The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry notes that swallowing fluoridated toothpaste can cause problems.
zh: 美国儿童牙科学会指出，吞咽含氟牙膏可能会带来问题。

text: So young children should be carefully supervised when they brush their teeth.
zh: 因此，幼儿刷牙时应有大人在旁仔细看护。

text: And only a small amount of fluoridated toothpaste the size of a pea should be used.
zh: 而且每次使用的含氟牙膏量应只有豌豆大小。

text: Parents often wonder what effect thumb sucking or sucking on a pacifier might have on their baby's teeth.
zh: 家长常常想知道吮吸拇指或安抚奶嘴会对宝宝的牙齿产生什么影响。

text: Dental experts generally agree that this is fine early in life.
zh: 牙科专家普遍认为，在孩子早期这样做是没问题的。

text: The American Academy of Family Physicians says most children stop sucking their thumb by the age of four.
zh: 美国家庭医生学会表示，大多数孩子在四岁前会自然停止吮吸拇指。

text: If it continues, the group advises parents to talk to their child's dentist or doctor.
zh: 如果这种习惯持续下去，该学会建议家长咨询孩子的牙医或医生。

text: It could interfere with the correct development of permanent teeth.
zh: 这可能会影响恒牙的正常发育。

text: Dentists say children should have their first dental visit at least by the time they are one year old.
zh: 牙医建议，孩子第一次看牙医的时间最迟不应晚于一岁。

text: They say babies should be examined when their first teeth appear, usually at around six months.
zh: 他们表示，婴儿应在第一颗牙齿长出时接受检查，通常是在大约六个月大的时候。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villareal.
zh: 我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔，为您带来美国之音特别英语报道。

text: You can learn English and much more at voaspecialenglish .com and the VOA Learning English page on Facebook.
zh: 您可以登录voaspecialenglish.com网站以及Facebook上的“美国之音学习英语”页面，学习英语及更多内容。
